_note: Sacrifice at Lu River and return from the southern campaign (late 225); Cao Pi dies, Cao Rui succeeds (226); Zhuge Liang's First Memorial on the northern expedition presented (spring 227). Xiahou Mao given command.
First Northern Expedition opens — Zhao Zilong's five-general feat at Fengming Ridge; Tianshui, Anding, Nanan all taken by Zhuge Liang's ruses.
Jiang Wei outwits and then defects to Zhuge Liang at Tianshui; Wang Lang scolded to death on the field.
Qiang allies defeated in a snowstorm; Sima Yi crushes Meng Da's revolt at Xincheng (Jan 228, very fast strike).
Ma Su loses Jieting to Zhang He; Zhuge Liang's 'empty city' lute ploy at Xicheng (Spring 228) covers the army's retreat.
Ma Su executed in tears; Zhou Fang cuts his hair to feign defection, luring Cao Xiu into the defeat at Shiting (autumn 228).
Cao Xiu's death post-Shiting; Zhuge Liang's Second Memorial; Second Northern Expedition besieges Chencang (winter 228 / spring 229).
Wang Shuang killed pursuing the Shu retreat; Third Northern Expedition (spring 229) takes Wudu and Yinping.
Text explicitly cites 'fourth month of Beginning Prosperity, seventh year (AD 229)'. Sima Yi takes overall command in the west; the Wei retaliatory invasion of Hanzhong is rained out (autumn 230).
Wei retreat through the Xie Valley; Fourth Northern Expedition opens (231) — Zhuge Liang's increasing-stoves ruse on the retreat home.
